steps.title: Identify what would support the resolution-dependency claim
steps.content: The researchers' conclusion states that variations in the landscape affect net CO₂ in ways that most models are too coarse to capture. To support this, a finding must show that FINER-resolution models (1 km²) and COARSER-resolution models (10 km²) produce different results for the same metric—proving that landscape variations matter and are lost in coarser grids.

steps.title: Match the resolution differences to the choices
steps.content: Choice C reports different heterotrophic respiration rates for autumn between the 1 km² and 10 km² models. This directly proves that landscape variations are captured differently at different resolutions, which is precisely what the conclusion claims. The other choices either describe seasonal patterns (A), report independence between variables (B), or show models reached identical conclusions despite different data (D)—none demonstrating that coarse models miss landscape information. That matches choice C — C is correct.
